Center: Center of Nuclear Physics Data, Russian Federal Nuclear Center (VNIIEF), Sarov, Russia
ref: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.180, p.615 (1972)
title: The 7Li(alpha,n)10B0 differential cross section for alpha-energies of up to 8 MeV

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.180, p.615 (1972)

x4codes.hlp: National Research Council, Ottawa, Ontario, Canada
National Research Council, Ottawa, Ontario, Canada

x4freetext: At energies below E = 5.51 MeV (threshold for n1
neutrons) the measurements were normalized to the
neutron production cross section of Gibbons et al.
later revised downwards by the same authors
7Li metal (0.007% 6Li) onto a tantalum backing. The
target thickness was between 50 and 150 ug/cm2
and selected such that for a particular run the maximum
alpha-energy beam spread did not exceed 100 keV. The
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
Proton recoil detector consisted either of a
stilbene crystal of 0.5 cm length and 2.5 cm diam., or
a NE 213 liquid scintillator with the following inside
dim.: length 0.63 cm, diam. 1.26 cm. The scintillators
were coupled to an RCA 8575 photomultiplier.
Crystal of 0.5 cm length and 2.5 cm diameter.
